title: School Nurse
subtitle: PreK-8
roleDescription: East Prairie School District 73 is seeking a dedicated School Nurse to oversee building-level health programs and medical compliance. This role is essential for managing student wellness, constructing individualized healthcare plans, and ensuring school-wide health and wellness requirements are met daily.
roleHighlight: Core Collaboration: You will work intimately with our Special Education Team to actively craft and advocate for medical 504 Plans and IEPs for students with medical needs.
cultureText: Our PreK-8 model lets you build multi-year relationships with families, watching kids grow from early childhood into resilient young adults. We believe a supportive, highly communicative environment brings out the best in our staff.
responsibilities: Provide compassionate clinical care for student medical emergencies, daily medications, and wellness checks.
Design and champion Individualized Healthcare Plans (IHPs) and Emergency Action Plans (EAPs) for students with chronic conditions.
Maintain regular, clear communication regarding student health needs with parents, teachers, and administration.
Ensure school-wide safety via precise health screenings, state immunization records, and strict adherence to FERPA and HIPAA standards.
Adapt flexibly to school needs and assist in the main office or with general student supervision when necessary.
requiredQuals: Minimum of a Bachelor's Degree in Nursing (BSN) from an accredited institution.
Current license as a Registered Professional Nurse (RN) in the State of Illinois.
preferredQuals: Illinois Professional Educator License (PEL) with a School Nurse Endorsement.
Previous clinical background in pediatric nursing, public health, or a school-based setting.
Strong clinical assessment skills and experience navigating IEP/504 processes.
benefits: Comprehensive Medical, Dental & Life Insurance plans
matchQuestions: I hold an active Illinois RN License.
I enjoy working in a dynamic environment with kids aged 3 to 14.
I understand or want to master the IEP/504 special education process.
